title: Front-Runner Best Actor Oscar Odds Change Dramatically - Newsweek
text: Timothée Chalamet looked nearly unstoppable just six weeks before the Oscars, and following his Golden Globe win for Marty Supreme on January 15, 2026, his chances of winning Best Actor surged to around 79 percent on prediction markets, making him the strongest front-runner in the category. Chalamet began the season as the frontrunner for his starring turn as table tennis protégé Marty Mauser in Marty Supreme, taking home the Golden Globe and the Critics Choice Award. However, the race underwent a dramatic reversal. Michael B. Jordan's odds spiked from single digits into the mid-30s, representing a major shake-up in the category. At the Actor Awards, Michael B. Jordan snagged the Best Actor trophy from frontrunner Timothée Chalamet. After missing out at BAFTA and SAG—the latter went to Michael B. Jordan—voters told Variety, He'll get one when he's older, suggesting the 30-year-old may have to wait longer. The result delivered a decisive blow to Timothée Chalamet's prospects for Marty Supreme, as his campaign style drew criticism from industry professionals and voters alike. Michael B. Jordan emerged as the new frontrunner after once trailing Timothée Chalamet and Leonardo DiCaprio. However, strong performances from Ethan Hawke, Timothée Chalamet, and Wagner Moura keep the category competitive.
